weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — all-new movements. Hypertrophy: chase reps and clean volume at moderate loads, not heavy singles. Everything here is fresh this week — read each card's cues before your first set, and never go past 3 sets on any pattern. Progress by moving a little better, not by adding a set.
testsLineHtml: Working number week. No formal test this week — tap your name below anytime to log a working number you want to track.

patterns.name: Comp-Pace Box Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · sit fully to the box and stand tall each rep
patterns.load: Your competition KB at competition box height
cues: Tap the box under control on every rep — do not drop or bounce off it.
Stand all the way up and squeeze your glutes at the top before the next rep.
Keep your chest up and knees tracking over your toes the whole set.
Breathe in on the way down and out as you stand — do not hold your breath.

patterns.name: Tempo Goblet Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 75 sec between sets · lower for a slow 3-count on every rep
patterns.load: A KB 1–2 sizes LIGHTER than your competition KB, held at the chest
cues: Take a full 3 seconds to lower so the muscles stay under tension.
Keep the bell tight against your chest and your elbows tucked in.
Sit between your knees, not back onto your heels, to stay tall.
Stand up at a normal speed — slow down only on the way down.

patterns.name: Step-Up to Knee Drive
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps each leg · rest 75 sec between sets · drive the back knee up at the top
patterns.load: Bodyweight, or a light KB held at the chest if bodyweight is easy
cues: Push through the whole foot on the box, not your toes, to stand up.
Drive the trailing knee up to hip height and pause for a beat.
Step down softly and quietly — do not let your foot slam down.
Use a box height where your working thigh is about parallel to start.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 throws · rest 75 sec between sets · 15 smooth throws total, technique over max effort
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men)
cues: Drop into a low hip hinge, then explode up and throw the ball up and back over your head. Let the hips do the work, not the arms.
Keep these throws smooth and repeatable this week — chase clean technique, not your farthest throw.
Reset your stance fully between every throw and take a breath before you load the next one.
Stop the set if your low back starts to arch — brace your stomach and keep your ribs stacked over your hips.

patterns.name: Romanian Deadlift — Tempo
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 75 sec between sets · lower for a slow 3-count each rep
patterns.load: A pair of DBs or one KB you can control for 12 clean reps with smooth form
cues: Push your hips straight back and slide the weights down your thighs until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, then stand tall by squeezing your glutes.
Lower for a slow 3-count and keep the weights close to your legs the whole way down.
Keep a soft bend in your knees and a flat back — don't round your shoulders or let your back hunch.
Stop the set the moment your back rounds; this builds the hinge, not your grip endurance.

patterns.name: Standing DB Overhead Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A pair of DBs light enough that rep 12 is still clean (no leaning back)
cues: Press both DBs straight overhead until your arms are locked out by your ears, then lower under control to your shoulders.
Brace your stomach and squeeze your glutes so your lower back stays flat — don't lean back to finish a rep.
Keep the reps smooth and controlled; this is volume work for the overhead position, not a max lift.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 3 sets · each set to ~65% of your max-rep test · rest 90 sec between · smooth controlled tempo
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — hit that number cleanly on all 3 sets and stop there, don't grind for extras.
Lower the bar to a light touch on your chest and press back up with a steady, controlled tempo.
End the set the instant the bar slows to a stall or your hips lift off the bench.

patterns.name: Incline DB Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Two DBs you can press for 12 clean reps with about 2 left in the tank
cues: Set a bench to a low incline, press both DBs straight up over your chest, and lock the arms out at the top.
Lower slowly until you feel a stretch across the front of your chest, then press back up — don't bang the DBs together.
Keep your feet flat and your back steady on the bench. Stop when your form starts to break down.

patterns.name: Banded Push-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight with a light resistance band looped across your upper back
cues: Loop a band over your back and under both hands so it adds tension as you press up — pick a band that lets you reach 15 clean reps.
Lower your chest with your elbows tracking back along your ribs, then press to a full lockout in one straight line.
Drop to your knees for the last reps rather than letting your hips sag. Quality over quantity.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 2 min between
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at ~80% of your current best hang time — stop the clock at 80%, do not chase a max today
cues: Pull your shoulders down away from your ears and stay active — this is not a relaxed dangle.
Breathe slow through your nose; never hold your breath under the bar.
Step down the moment your grip starts to fail rather than dropping suddenly.
Log the time you held so the app can set your next target.

patterns.name: Fat-Grip Bent-Over Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Dumbbells you can row for a clean 15 with 2 reps left in the tank · fat grips on the handles
cues: Hinge to a flat back, chest proud, and let the fat grips force your hand to work the whole set.
Pull the dumbbells to your hip pockets and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top.
Lower under control for a 2-second count; do not let the weight yank your arms straight.
If the fat grips slip before 15, that rep counts — finish what your grip allows.

patterns.name: Scap Pull-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight hanging from the bar — band-assist under one foot if you cannot reach 12
cues: Hang at full stretch, then pull only your shoulder blades down to lift your body an inch — arms stay straight.
Pause at the top and feel the lats switch on before lowering.
Move slowly and smoothly; this is a small range, not a swing.
If you cannot get 12, loop a light band under one foot to assist.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball
cues: Stand tall, reach the ball fully overhead, then slam it straight down between your feet.
Catch the bounce and reset each rep — don't rush and lose your full overhead reach.
Keep volume smooth and controlled; this is muscle-building reps, not a race.

patterns.name: Tall-Kneeling D-Ball Slam
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A light D-ball, 10 lb (women) / 20 lb (men)
cues: Kneel upright on a pad with hips locked out, then slam straight down in front of your knees.
Drive the slam with your lats and trunk since your legs are taken out of it.
Stay tall — don't let your butt sink back toward your heels as you tire.

patterns.name: Lat Pulldown
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A moderate stack you can control for 15 clean reps
cues: Pull the bar to your upper chest by driving your elbows down, then return slow to a full stretch.
Squeeze your shoulder blades down and back; don't lean way back and heave the weight.
This builds the pulling muscles that finish every slam.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 60 sec of singles · rest 90 sec between sets · steady, repeatable pace
patterns.load: Your jump rope, at a controlled comp pace
cues: Turn the rope with your wrists, not big arm circles, so you can hold the pace for the full 60 seconds.
Jump just an inch or two off the floor — barely clear the rope rather than bouncing high.
Count your singles each set and aim to repeat the same number, not sprint and burn out.

patterns.name: Seated Weighted Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 3 × 24 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · 2-sec hold at the top
patterns.load: Heavy DB or KB resting on your knees — heavy enough that 24 reps is hard but doable
cues: Press all the way up onto the balls of your feet and squeeze the calf at the top.
Lower slowly until you feel a stretch at the bottom — don't bounce off the bottom.
Keep the weight settled on your thighs, not sliding toward your knees.

patterns.name: Single-Leg Standing Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 3 × 16 reps each leg · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight holding one light DB on the working side; hold a rail with the other hand
cues: Stand tall on one foot and rise all the way onto the ball of that foot.
Hold a wall or rail for balance only — let the calf do the work, not your arm.
Control the way down for a full count instead of dropping fast.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 jumps · rest 90 sec between sets · smooth and controlled
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Swing your arms back, then jump forward for distance and stick the landing on both feet.
Bend your knees and hips to absorb each landing softly — this week is about clean, controlled landings, not max reach.
Step back to the line and reset before every jump. Stop the set if your landings get sloppy.

patterns.name: Goblet Box Step-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps each leg · rest 75 sec between sets · controlled up, slow down
patterns.load: A KB or single DB held at your chest, heavy enough that 12 reps per leg is challenging on a knee-height box
cues: Plant your whole foot on the box and drive through that heel to stand tall — don't push off your back toe.
Lower yourself slowly back down under control; tap the floor and go again rather than bouncing.
Keep your chest up and the bell tight to your body. Hold a rail with one hand if your balance wavers.

patterns.name: Glute Bridge March
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 reps total · rest 60 sec between sets · 2-sec hold each lift
patterns.load: Bodyweight, lying on your back with knees bent and feet flat
cues: Squeeze your glutes to lift your hips into a straight bridge, then lift one knee toward your chest without letting the hips drop.
Brace your stomach so your low back doesn't arch — the work is in your glutes, not your spine.
Move slowly and alternate legs. Set both feet down and rest if your hips start to sag.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 500m · rest 3 min fully between · row at a strong, repeatable effort
patterns.load: Rower · about 4–6 sec/500m slower than an all-out 500m so all three pieces match
cues: Settle into a pace you can hit on all three pieces without fading on the last one.
Drive with the legs first, then swing the back, then pull the handle to the ribs — in that order every stroke.
Keep the stroke rate around 24–26 and breathe in rhythm with the recovery — don't hold your breath.

patterns.name: Seated Cable Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A cable stack weight heavy enough that the last 2 reps are hard at 15
cues: Sit tall, pull the handle to your belly button, and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the back.
Let the handle travel back out under control for a full count — don't let the stack yank your arms forward.
Keep your torso quiet; row with your back and arms, not by rocking from the hips.

patterns.name: Long Steady Bike Engine
patterns.rx: 1 × 12 min continuous · easy, steady spin
patterns.load: Stationary bike · a conversational pace you could hold and still talk
cues: Pedal at an easy, even effort the whole way — you should be able to chat through it.
Keep your cadence smooth and steady; this builds the engine without pounding the joints.
Sit tall and relaxed — ease off if your breathing gets ragged.

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 200-yd shuttle (4 × 50 yd) · rest 3 min between · controlled comp-tempo turns
patterns.load: Bodyweight · run at a steady comp-rehearsal pace you could hold for the full 300, not a sprint
cues: Run four 50-yard lengths at the pace you plan to hold on comp day, decelerating a step before each turn.
Plant low and push back off smoothly — no hard cuts since no one is spotting you.
Walk and breathe for the full 3 minutes between sets so each one stays clean and repeatable.

patterns.name: Reverse Lunge Walk
patterns.rx: 3 × 16 reps (8 per leg) · slow controlled tempo · rest 90 sec
patterns.load: Bodyweight, or hold one 15–25 lb dumbbell at your chest if bodyweight feels easy
cues: Step back into a long lunge, lower your back knee toward the floor under control, then drive up tall.
Keep your front shin vertical and your weight in the front heel — don't let the knee cave inward.
Move slowly for muscle, not speed; finish all 16 reps with good form before resting.

patterns.name: Marching High-Knee Drill
patterns.rx: 3 × 30 yards · brisk march · rest 60–90 sec
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: March forward driving each knee up to hip height with a tall posture and pumping arms.
Stay light and rhythmic on the balls of your feet — this builds the leg turnover you need to shuttle.
Keep the volume high and the effort moderate; this is capacity work, not a sprint.

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 3 × 50 yards · rest 90 sec between · steady, controlled (not timed)
patterns.load: Competition load: 2 × 25 lb plates (women) / 2 × 45 lb plates (men)
cues: Push the full 50 yards at a steady, controlled pace — this is volume work, not a max sprint and not timed.
Hold your body at about a 45° angle with arms locked, and push through your trunk — don't bend your elbows to absorb the load.
Take long steps with full hip extension and drive through your toes the whole way.

patterns.name: Goblet Reverse Lunge
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps per leg · controlled tempo · rest 75 sec between
patterns.load: One dumbbell or kettlebell held at your chest, heavy enough that the last 2 reps per leg are tough but clean
cues: Step straight back into a lunge and lower under control, then drive up through your front heel to build the same leg power the sled needs.
Keep your chest tall and the weight at your sternum — don't let your shoulders round forward.
Pause for a beat at the bottom so each rep is quality, and stop the set if your form starts to break down.

patterns.name: DB Suitcase Carry
patterns.rx: 3 × 50 ft (25 ft down and back) per side · rest 75 sec between
patterns.load: One dumbbell in one hand, heavy enough that your grip and trunk are working hard by the end of the walk
cues: Carry the weight in one hand and walk tall and steady — your free side will want to tip, so brace your core to stay upright.
Take controlled steps and squeeze the handle hard to build the grip and bracing that holds your push position.
Set the weight down and switch hands between trips — don't let the load drag your shoulder down.
